title: SX1276 LoRa Radio Module (RFM95W)
design_question: How to transmit sensor data from a remote outdoor node over several kilometres with minimal power consumption?
selected_approach: HopeRF RFM95W (Semtech SX1276 core) 868 MHz / 915 MHz LoRa module. SPI interface; 3.3 V; +20 dBm output. Configured via Arduino LoRa library (Sandeep Mistry): spreading factor SF10, bandwidth 125 kHz, coding rate 4/5. Gateway: same module or LoRaWAN-compatible gateway (RAK7268). Node transmits 50-byte payload, then sleeps.
rationale: LoRa achieves 2–5 km range in urban areas and up to 15 km line-of-sight on 868 MHz at SF10 with < 40 mA transmit current. No WiFi infrastructure required. SX1276 is the de-facto standard for DIY long-range sensor nodes. RFM95W module is £4–6 and readily available.

alternatives.reason_rejected: 100 m range only; unsuitable for field stations more than 50 m from the base.
Requires SIM card, ongoing data cost, draws 2 A peak — incompatible with solar power budget.

verification_notes: FACT: 'LoRa / LoRaWAN — LPWAN for Remote Telemetry' confirms 2–15 km range, SF7–SF12, and 40 mA Tx current for SX1276.
supporting_fact_titles: LoRa / LoRaWAN — LPWAN for Remote Telemetry
